title: The Unlabeled Hour
logline: A decanted degreaser with no label and no PPE meets a loose cap in receiving.

beats.heading: INT. RECEIVING AREA - MORNING
beats.description: Drums and cartons stack the receiving area. On a cart, an unlabeled container of degreaser - decanted last shift, never marked - waits beside the manifest it does not appear on.
beats.vo: Every container tells you what it is. This one was told to keep quiet.

beats.heading: INT. RECEIVING BENCH - CONTINUOUS
beats.description: A technician handles the container bare-handed, face uncovered. As Priya lifts it, the loose cap lets go and solvent splashes across her forearm and cheek. The spill kit hangs by the door, seconds away.
beats.vo: The label was missing. The gloves were optional. The cap was loose. Pick any one to change the morning.

beats.heading: INT. SAFETY STATION / RECEIVING BENCH - LATER
beats.description: Priya at the eyewash station, water running the full fifteen minutes. Corrective replay: the same transfer with a labeled container, ppe gloves on, face shield down, cap torqued.
beats.vo: Fifteen minutes at the eyewash. Fifteen seconds to label the bottle.

rule_card: Label every transfer. Gloves and face shield before you lift. Fifteen minutes at the eyewash - no shortcuts.
